steps.title: Find the yearly multiplier
steps.content:
To find the factor by which the value changes each year, evaluate the function at $x=12$ months (one year).
$$ f(12)=3950(0.38)^{12/12}=3950(0.38)^1=3950\cdot0.38 $$
This shows that after one year the value is $0.38$ times its value at the start of the year.

steps.title: Compute the annual percent decrease
steps.content:
If the value each year is $0.38$ of the previous year's value, then it has decreased by $1-0.38=0.62$ of the previous year's value.
$$ \text{Decrease fraction}=0.62 $$
Converting to a percentage gives $p=0.62\times100=62$.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
